text:
If you needed any further proof of the superhuman abilities of the Olympians competing at this summer’s Games inParis, look no further…

The women’s rugby sevens competition has been a compelling and brutal watch throughout, with scintillating plays, free-flowing moves and huge hits on display across the rounds in the French capital.

And while Ireland’s campaign came to an end with a disappointing seventh-place play-off defeat at the hands of Team GB, there was one incredible moment from the performance which left viewers’ jaws on the floor.

Holding an imperious 28-5 lead with just five minutes remaining on the clock, Team GB kicked off and sent the ball looping into Ireland’s half in the direction of Emily Lane, who appeared to slightly misjudge its trajectory.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

Thankfully, Erin King – who hoisted Lane aloft – had the presence of mind and sheer strength to save her team-mate from certain injury by somehow managing to prevent the scrum-half from toppling backwards, head first into the turf.

The almighty grimace on King’s face showed just how much grit and determination it took to ensure her fellow countrywoman avoided a nasty fall from a great height.

Replays of the moments immediately began doing the rounds across social media and King has received universal praise, especially given the fact Ireland were well and truly out of the contest by that stage.

I found a longer version of the clip:pic.twitter.com/YDeGrrrWEZ

Ultimately, indiscipline contributed to Ireland’s downfall against Team GB, with Allan Temple-Jones’ side losing two players – Stacey Flood andKing – to the sin bin during the heavy 28-12 loss.

Meg Jones and Heather Cowell both crossed over for Team GB, while Claire Boles marked her Olympic debut with a late consolation try for Ireland after Megan Burns’ earlier effort.

‘We’ve just got to learn. Playing in front of so many people at the Stade de France is unbelievable for us,’ Ireland head coach Temple-Jones said.

First time in the Olympics, first time for a lot of these young girls to get a go.

‘We’re going to take lots of learnings from it moving forward. We’ve got to grow the squad and grow the depth of our group.’

He added: ‘Exposing young girls to an Olympics like this and a stadium like this is going to be strength for both the 15s and Sevens squads, it’s brilliant.

‘To get girls playing on this stage and mixing between both squads on the 15s and Sevens side is unbelievable.

‘We hope the youngsters grow from this and move forward and be back in Los Angeles in four years’ time.’

title: Ireland rugby star shows superhuman strength to save team-mate from certain injury at Olympics | Metro News
description: One Olympic Rugby Sevens star showcased the superhuman abilities of Olympians competing at the 2024 games in Paris.
keywords: Sport,Ireland Rugby Team,Los Angeles,Olympics,Paris,Republic of Ireland,Rugby,Team GB
